page: Google Cloud Platform c4d-highcpu-8 is a compute-optimized virtual machine belonging to the c4d server family. It features 8 dedicated vCPUs built on the x86_64 CPU architecture. The instance is configured with 15.0 GB of system memory, yielding a memory-to-core ratio of 1.88 GB per vCPU.

This server configuration does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, meaning all storage requirements must be met via network-attached block storage. Additionally, it does not include any complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

The dedicated CPU allocation ensures consistent processing performance without resource contention. Given the low memory-to-core ratio, this instance is qualitatively cost-efficient for compute-heavy workloads that do not require large memory footprints. Suitable workloads include batch processing, web serving, distributed analytics, and compute-intensive microservices.
description: Google Cloud Platform c4d-highcpu-8 is an x86_64 compute-optimized virtual machine from the c4d server family. It is configured with 8 dedicated vCPUs and 15.0 GB of system memory, providing a resource density of 1.88 GB of RAM per core. This server does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, requiring network-attached storage for data persistence. It also provides zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses. With its dedicated CPU allocation, the instance delivers consistent processing capabilities. The low memory-to-core ratio makes it a qualitatively cost-effective option for compute-bound tasks that do not demand large memory capacities. It is suited for workloads such as batch processing, web serving, microservices, and distributed compute tasks.
